behavior_summary: This script implements a simple in-game winch object. When examined (U8P_WINCHEW::look), it emits a short bark 'winch'.

When used (U8P_WINCHEW::use), it checks the item's quality via Item::getQuality(this). If quality == 0 the object is treated as locked: a 'locked' SFX is played and the bark 'locked' is emitted, then the U8P_WINCHEW::use process ends.

If usable (quality != 0), U8P_WINCHEW::use clears an internal engine reference (FREE::resetRef with process type 0x0205) and invokes U8P_WINCHEW::func0110 to run the winch operation/animation sequence.

U8P_WINCHEW::func0110 appears to drive the visible/temporal operation: it runs nested iteration counters, plays an operation SFX at each outer iteration, steps item frames via Item::setFrame in an inner loop, spawns or schedules a helper via FREE::func1C19, suspends to allow timed progression, and increments counters until thresholds are exceeded. The result is an animated, time-stepped winch operation with sound and visual frame updates.
notable_control_flow: Guard on usability in U8P_WINCHEW::use: quality = Item::getQuality(this); if quality == 0 -> playSFX + bark 'locked' -> end; else -> FREE::resetRef(...) -> call U8P_WINCHEW::func0110.

U8P_WINCHEW::func0110 uses a two-level loop pattern: outer counter local1 (0..<=2 inferred) controlling operation cycles, and inner counter local2 (0..<=3 inferred) stepping frames with Item::setFrame and spawning/scheduling FREE::func1C19 between suspended ticks.

Temporal progression is implemented with suspend points inside the inner loop (inferred from suspend nodes and spawn of FREE::func1C19), producing a multi-frame animated sequence rather than an instantaneous change.
open_questions: What exact sound effects correspond to the numeric parameters passed to playSFX (0x64h, 0x5Ah, 0x008Ch)? (SFX meaning is not present in this excerpt.)
What does FREE::func1C19(0) specifically spawn or schedule (visual effect, damage, callback)?
What exact semantics does FREE::resetRef(0x0205h, this) have for this object/process type (clearing listeners, stopping other processes, or resetting an engine slot)?

Are there any external global flags or broader game state changes triggered by the winch operation outside the visible local behavior (none are written in the excerpt but higher-level effects may exist)?

main_function.name: U8P_WINCHEW::look
main_function.purpose: Present the object's short examine description to the player: emit the bark 'winch' and end the U8P_WINCHEW::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player examines or 'looks at' the object (the engine invokes the U8P_WINCHEW::look process).

function_map.name: U8P_WINCHEW::use
function_map.role: U8P_WINCHEW::use/activate handler (checks usability, gives locked feedback, starts operation)

function_map.name: U8P_WINCHEW::func0110
function_map.role: operation/animation sequencing (plays SFX, advances frames, spawns timed callbacks, uses suspend to time steps)

internal_calls.purpose: Transfer control to the operation/animation sequence after confirming the item is usable and resetting engine references.

external_calls.purpose: Query the item's quality/state; used as the guard to decide if the winch is locked (quality == 0) or usable.
Play a 'locked' feedback sound when quality == 0. Numeric parameters present in the raw indicate a specific SFX variant.
Clear or reset an engine-level reference/state (process type 0x0205) associated with this object before starting the operation.
Play the operation SFX at the start of each outer animation cycle to provide audible feedback during the winch operation.
Advance the visual frame/state of the item (inner loop frame steps) to display the winch animation.
Spawn or schedule a delayed helper/callback (likely to continue animation effects or produce a timed visual/audio effect during the inner loop).
Engine-level operation used in combination with FREE::func1C19 to schedule a separate process; inferred from spawn ->FREE::func1C19(0) remnants.
Yield the process to allow time to pass between frame steps; used to implement temporal spacing of animation frames.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a simple in-game winch object. When examined (U8P_WINCHEW::look), it emits a short bark 'winch'.
